meta.title: centr

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: centr
root.mentor_note: centr always points toward a middle, whether physical, political, or astronomical.

attempt.direction_prompt: Far from the mainstream, or firmly in the middle?

context_sentences: The new leader positioned herself as a centrist, careful to alienate neither wing of the party.
His voting record marked him as a centrist, siding with neither bloc more than about half the time.
Their eccentric uncle kept a collection of antique typewriters in every room of the house.
The professor’s eccentric habit of lecturing in verse became the department’s favourite story.
His egocentric habit of steering every conversation back to himself wore the team down within weeks.
Small children are naturally egocentric long before they learn to consider another person’s perspective.

construct_options.text: Excessively focused on oneself, at the expense of others
Located exactly at the physical middle of a space
Unconventional or strange in an amusing way

context_sentences: Accepting the heliocentric model cost the astronomer his standing with the church for the rest of his life.
Long before it could be proven, a few thinkers had already guessed the universe was heliocentric.

construct_options.text: Having the sun as the centre, especially of the solar system
Having the Earth as the fixed centre of the universe
Producing intense heat and bright light
